objects.name: PAP
objects.description: This defines PAP as a STIX extension

objects.name: Central and Eastern Europe Espionage
objects.description: Exploitation of an MSHTML zero-day (CVE-2026-21513) to bypass security controls and execute code via malicious LNK and HTML files.

objects.name: CVE-2026-21513
objects.description: Vulnerability CVE-2026-21513 referenced in threat report.

objects.content:
Sector: Government, Defense

Modus operandi:
Spear-phishing with weaponized LNK files bypassing Mark of the Web (MotW). Use of wellnesscaremed[.]com infrastructure.

objects.name: Ruby Jumper
objects.description: Infiltration of air-gapped networks using multi-stage malware (THUMBSBD, SNAKEDROPPER) delivered via USB and using cloud services like Zoho WorkDrive for C2.

objects.content:
Sector: Government, Defense, South Korea-related entities

Modus operandi:
Use of Ruby-based environment (SNAKEDROPPER) disguised as USB utilities. THUMBSBD manages data exfiltration from isolated systems via hidden folders on removable media.

objects.name: The Com Network Operations
objects.description: The broader Com network, including ShinyHunters and Scattered Spider, targeted retailers and premium users of various platforms (Odido, Pornhub).

objects.content:
Sector: Telecommunications, Retail, Adult Entertainment

Modus operandi:
Data theft and extortion via social engineering and credential harvesting.

objects.name: CVE-2026-0628
objects.description:
Insufficient policy enforcement in WebView tag allowed malicious extensions to hijack the Gemini Live panel, gaining access to camera, microphone, and local files.

Affected product: Google Chrome

Affected vendor: Google

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:google:chrome:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2025-64328
objects.description:
Command injection vulnerability in the Endpoint Manager filestore module. Exploited to deploy EncystPHP web shells on over 900 systems.

Affected product: FreePBX

Affected vendor: Sangoma

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:sangoma:freepbx:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: Breach: Wynn Resorts
objects.description:
Exfiltration of HR-related data and employee records following an extortion threat. Operations were reportedly not disrupted.

Sector: Hospitality, Casino

objects.name: Breach: Odido
objects.description:
Major data breach involving millions of customer records leaked by the ShinyHunters group.

Sector: Telecommunications

objects.abstract: Geopolitical analysis (EN) / Iran-Israel Military Conflict (Operation Epic Fury)
objects.content:
Sector: Energy, Maritime, Critical Infrastructure

Theme: Iran-Israel Military Conflict (Operation Epic Fury)

The killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has triggered immediate kinetic and cyber retaliation across the Middle East. Threats focus on maritime transit (Strait of Hormuz) and critical infrastructure in Israel, USA, and Gulf states.

Tags: Middle-East, Iran, War

objects.name: CyberStrikeAI Hacking Tool Adoption
objects.description:
An AI-native security testing platform built in Go, adopted by threat actors for automated vulnerability discovery and attack orchestration against edge devices like FortiGate.

Analysis:
CyberStrikeAI lowers the barrier for low-skilled attackers by automating the full attack chain (Nmap, Metasploit, etc.) via AI agents.

Recommendations:
- Monitor and block the identified IP 212.11.64.250.
- Review edge device exposure and patch known vulnerabilities in VPN/Firewall appliances.

objects.name: UAC-0252 Phishing and Stealer Campaign
objects.description:
Distribution of SHADOWSNIFF and SALATSTEALER malwares via spoofed government emails and XSS-vulnerable websites.

Analysis:
UAC-0252 uses social engineering by prompting users to update official mobile apps (Diia) to deliver info-stealers.

Recommendations:
- Implement strict filtering for GitHub-hosted executable files.
- Educate users about fake application update alerts.

objects.content: The cyber threat landscape on March 3, 2026, is dominated by the massive escalation of the conflict in the Middle East following 'Operation Epic Fury', leading to immediate retaliatory cyber activity from Iranian state-sponsored and hacktivist groups (Handala Hack, Cyber Islamic Resistance). Simultaneously, a critical trend is emerging in the weaponization of Artificial Intelligence: hackers are adopting AI-native platforms like CyberStrikeAI to automate attack chains, while high-severity vulnerabilities in AI-integrated products (Chrome Gemini, Claude Code, OpenClaw) are being actively disclosed or exploited. Furthermore, Russian APT28 remains highly active, exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-21513) for geopolitical espionage in Europe.
